caption: A sequence of screen captures shows a Linux terminal window running on a dark-themed operating system. A user interacts with a Kubernetes cluster using command-line tools. In the first frame, the user executes `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces`, displaying a list of pods including `coredns`, `kube-apiserver-minikube`, and `storage-provisioner` all in a "Running" status. As the sequence progresses, the user enters `kubectl create -f pod-apache-annotated.yaml`, which deploys a new pod named `apache-logo-annotated`. The subsequent `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces` command confirms this new pod is also "Running". Finally, the user begins typing `kubectl logs`, targeting the newly created pod. The top bar of the desktop displays the date "Oct 28" and the time shifting from "00:20" to "00:21", while the terminal window remains centered against a dark blue background.
